Job Description

We are looking for reliable part-time callers with strong English communication skills to contact credit unions and Experian on a daily basis. This role requires confidence on the phone, attention to detail, and consistency in follow-ups.

The ideal candidate will be comfortable making outbound calls, documenting conversations accurately, and following company scripts and procedures when communicating with financial institutions and credit bureaus.

Responsibilities

Call credit unions to follow up on credit-related inquiries





Contact Experian daily regarding dispute status, verifications, and account updates





Clearly communicate and document call outcomes





Follow company-provided scripts and procedures





Maintain accurate records of calls and results





Coordinate updates with internal tea
mbers when needed





Follow company procedures and compliance guidelines





Work Monitoring & Time Tracking

To ensure accurate time reporting and productivity transparency, all tea
mbers are required to use the company’s time-tracking and activity monitoring system while performing work for Wolf Valley Funding Co..

The monitoring system must be installed on the device used for company work.





Tea
mbers must start the timer when beginning work on company tasks and stop the timer when ending their work session.





Activity monitoring and screenshots are recorded only during active tracked work sessions.





Tracked time should reflect dedicated work performed for Wolf Valley Funding Co.





Participation in the monitoring and time-tracking system is a required condition for this role.





Confidentiality & Client Data Protection

Because this role involves handling sensitive consumer credit information, all tea
mbers must follow strict confidentiality and data protection standards.

Client information, credit reports, and personal data must be kept strictly confidential at all times.





Employees may not download, copy, store, or share client data outside authorized company systems.





Credit-related information may only be accessed for legitimate work purposes related to assigned client accounts.





Any misuse, disclosure, or unauthorized sharing of client data may result in immediate termination of engagement.





Performance Expectations

To maintain service quality and workflow efficiency, tea

You will work directly with the business owner and leadership team to identify opportunities where AI and automation can improve operations, sales processes, and administration.



This is a long-term role for someone who enjoys building systems that make businesses run better.



What You Will Be Doing



Your role will involve designing and building automation tools such as:



AI agents that read and process incoming
s



Automating CRM workflows and sales pipelines



Building bots that create jobs or tasks when plans or documents are received



Connecting business systems (CRM,
, project management tools)



Creating internal dashboards and reporting automation



Developing small AI assistants for our internal team



Removing repetitive admin tasks through automation



Continuously improving systems and workflows across the business



The goal is to make the company more efficient every month by replacing manual work with smart automation.



Example Projects You May Work On



Some examples of projects include:



• AI inbox assistant that categorises
s and drafts replies

• Automation that detects new quote requests and creates jobs in our systems

• Follow-up automation for sales quotes

• Automated reporting dashboards for sales and operations

• AI assistant that helps staff find information quickly

• Integration between CRM,
, and project systems



Required Skills



You should have experience in some of the following areas:



Python



AI APIs (OpenAI / GPT / Claude or similar)



Automation tools such as Zapier or Make



API integrations



CRM automation



 workflow automation



Basic database and scripting knowledge



Strong problem solving and the ability to design systems independently is very important.



Nice To Have



Experience with:



Microsoft tools (Outlook, Teams)



Building AI agents



Workflow automation



Construction or service businesses



What We Are Looking For



We are looking for someone who:



Enjoys building systems and solving problems



Can identify automation opportunities independently



Writes clean, reliable code



Communicates well in English



Wants to grow with the company long term



About Us



We are an Australian construction company specialising in roofing and architectural cladding for residential and commercial projects. We are focused on improving our systems and scaling the business using technology and automation.



How To Apply



When applying, please include:



Examples of automations or AI systems you have built



Tools and programming languages you use



A short explanation of how you would automate a busy business inbox

title: Real Estate Media Post-Production Manager & Virtual Assistant
description:
We are seeking a highly organized Post-Production Manager & VA to oversee the end-to-end workflow of our real estate photography business. You will be the bridge between our three field photographers, our editing tools, and our clients. You will manage orders within HDPhotoHub, oversee quality control for photos and videos, and ensure every "add-on" (like virtual twilights or floor plans) is uploaded accurately and on time.



This role begins with high-level photo editing and order management, offering the opportunity to expand into Social Media Management as you master our internal systems.



Key Responsibilities



1. Order & Workflow Management (HDPhotoHub)



Logistics: Monitor HDPhotoHub daily to track new orders and ensure every line item (photos, floor plans, Zillow tours, etc.) is accounted for.



Data Tracking: Maintain internal spreadsheets to track project status, video editor assignments, and delivery deadlines.



File Routing: Organize and share assets via Dropbox with external video editors and ensure returned files are placed in the correct property folders.



2. Photo Editing & Enhancement (AutoHDR & Adobe)



AutoHDR Processing: After photographers upload all images to AutoHDR for processing, modify edits using fine-tuning sliders (temp, brightness, sharpness, contrast, etc.) to achieve a consistent look.



Specialized Edits: Perform or oversee "add-on" edits



Green Grass Replacement and Virtual Twilights (created in AutoHDR)



Fireplace inserts for luxury properties (created in AutoHDR)



Boundary Lines: Use Adobe software to draw precise property lines on aerial photos.



Final QC: Conduct a final quality check for color, distortion correction, advanced object removal (if requested, e.g., clutter, cords), and image reordering before uploading to Dropbox/HDPhotoHub



3. Video Quality Assurance



Review: Watch all real estate highlight videos to ensure they are free of glitches, exported correctly, and maintain high production standards.



Proofreading: Check all on-screen text, lower thirds, and titles for spelling and grammatical errors (e.g., agent names, addresses).



Delivery: Ensure video files link correctly within the HDPhotoHub delivery system.



4. Social Media & Brand Growth (Expansion Phase)



Content Creation: Once comfortable with the production workflow, utilize Canva to create engaging posts, "Just Listed" graphics, and "Before & After" reels.



Platform Management: Schedule and post content across
, Instagram, and TikTok to showcase our photographers' best work.



Engagement: Help maintain the brand voice and interact with our community of realtors.



Qualifications & Skills



Technical Proficiency: Experience with Adobe Photoshop is required. Familiarity with HDPhotoHub, AutoHDR, Dropbox, and Canva is a significant advantage. 



Detail Obsession: You must be the type of person who notices a typo in a 2-second clip or a crooked wall in a photo.



Communication: Excellent written English for proofreading and internal coordination.



Multitasking: Ability to manage multiple "moving parts" (photos, videos, floor plans) for 3 different photographers simultaneously.



Working Conditions



Remote/Work from Home

Training: 7am -10am (PHT), Tuesday-Saturday, first two weeks, using Google Meet

Once Trained: Work can be begin no earlier than 7am (PHT), but all work must be completed by 8pm (PHT), Tuesday -Saturday

s, and Gen Z humor

title: Construction Virtual Assistant — Contracts, Client Communication & Invoicing (US Construction Experience Required)
description:
Job Title: Construction Virtual Assistant — Contracts, Client Communication & Invoicing (US Construction Experience Required)



Hours: Part-Time, Flexible 4-8 hours/day | Must be available 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Pacific Standard Time (PST)



Rate: $4/hour



About Us:

Drams Construction is a California-based construction company working on residential and commercial projects in the San Francisco Bay Area and Los Angeles. We're looking for a Virtual Assistant with hands-on experience supporting US construction operations — someone who already understands how construction projects work in the United States, from contracts to change orders to collecting payments.

This is not a general VA role. You need to know construction.



What You'll Do:

- Communicate directly with US-based clients via
 and phone — project updates, scheduling, follow-ups, and answering questions

- Prepare, send, and track owner-contractor agreements and subcontractor agreements using Company templates

- Send contracts out for e-signature (DocuSign or similar) and follow up until fully executed

- Prepare and process change orders as directed by management

- Create and send invoices to clients

- Follow up on accounts receivable — track outstanding payments, send reminders, and escalate past-due balances

- Prepare and track pay applications, lien waivers, and related construction payment documents

- Collect and organize job site photos from contractors, subcontractors, and field teams

- Maintain and update CRM with project status, client details, contract milestones, and communication logs

- Manage construction project files and documents in Google Drive

- General administrative support — scheduling, data entry, document formatting, coordination with subcontractors and vendors



You Must Have:

- Experience working with a US-based construction company — this is required, not optional. You must understand how construction projects operate in the United States, including permitting, contracts, payment workflows, and client expectations

- Knowledge of construction documents: owner-contractor agreements, subcontractor agreements, AIA contracts, change orders, pay applications, lien waivers, notices to owner, and certificates of insurance

- Excellent written and spoken English — you will be speaking and writing directly to US-based clients, general contractors, and subcontractors daily. Professional, confident communication is essential

- Experience with CRM software (Airtable, ClickUp, HubSpot, Monday, Salesforce, Zoho, or similar)

- Comfortable creating invoices and tracking accounts receivable

- Strong organizational skills — ability to manage multiple active projects and deadlines at the same time without dropping anything

- Experience with Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Calendar)

- Familiarity with e-signature platforms (Google Signature)

- Reliable high-speed internet connection

- Available during 9 AM – 5 PM PST (flexible within this window)



Nice to Have:

- Construction manage degree

- Experience with QuickBooks Online (invoicing, payment tracking)

- Experience in residential construction specifically

- Familiarity with California construction requirements (mechanics liens, preliminary notices, contractor licensing)

- Experience with architecture or real estate in addition to construction

- Experience with project management tools (ClickUp, Procore, Buildertrend, or similar)



What We Offer:

- Flexible part-time schedule (4-8 hours/day depending on workload)

- Consistent, long-term work with room to grow into more hours

- Small, close-knit team where your work directly impacts the business

- Payment via Wise, processed on time every pay period

- Time tracked via Hubstaff



How to Apply:

Your application MUST include all four of the following. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

1. Loom Video (required): Record a 2-3 minute Loom video (

title: Full-time Social Media Manager
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Long-Term

42 hours/week | 6 days/week

$1,000 – $1,400 USD/month (based on experience)



Who This Role Is For:



This role is for someone who genuinely loves social media and understands how Instagram works today (trends, formats, timing, and why certain posts go viral)



You don’t need years of professional experience!

What we care about most is your passion for content creation and your understanding of what makes content trend and perform well.



If you’re the type who notices patterns on your feed, saves posts for “content inspo,” or can explain why a Reel popped off then this role will be a great fit.



Your responsibilities include:

- Posting content daily across multiple Instagram accounts

- Writing captions and stories that feel natural and engaging

- Monitoring post performance (reach, saves, shares, engagement)

- Making smart posting decisions based on data and patterns

- Spotting trending formats and adapting them quickly

- Reviewing content carefully before posting to avoid mistakes

- Sharing insights with the content team on what’s working and what’s not



You’ll be trained on how to analyze metrics, understand posting patterns, and improve performance over time.



What We’re Looking For:

- Strong interest in social media and content creation

- Good understanding of social media trends and viral content

- Attention to detail and strong judgment

- Comfortable working independently in a remote setup

- Willingness to learn and improve

- Experience is a plus BUT NOT REQUIRED

- Personal pages, passion projects, or content you’ve made for fun all count as experience.



Why You’ll Like This Role:

- Fully remote work

- No micromanagement

- Paid training and regular feedback

- Opportunity to work on accounts with large audiences

- Clear growth path into strategist or leadership roles

- Supportive and healthy team culture



How to Apply (Please Read Carefully)

To be considered, your application must include:

1. A portfolio (this can be client work, personal Instagram pages, Reels, TikToks, or any creative projects.)

2. A short video introduction (1–2 minutes): Tell us why you’re a great fit. Be creative &  show your personality. We are not looking for a boring or scripted intro.

3. The number “100” in your application subject line (this helps us know you read the full post)



IMPORTANT!!!!!!!

*No video introduction = no review

*Boring or low-effort video = automatic pass



We’re looking for someone creative, detail-oriented, and passionate about social media not just someone who can follow instructions.



If this sounds like you, apply. We’re excited to see what you bring!

title: Long-term PHP + Angular Developer
description:
We’re looking for a skilled and enthusiastic developer to join a small, fast-paced remote team working on an established platform.



You will collaborate with both a senior and a junior developer based in different parts of the world and work directly with the client in Australia. For this reason, excellent spoken and written English is required, and you must be available between 9am and 5pm Manila time. Full-time work is not required but may be available to the right applicant. In either case, flexibility in your availability is important.



Tech stack



Frontend

Angular 19

TypeScript

Bootstrap



Backend

PHP 8.3

Laravel

MySQL

Nginx

Ubuntu



Infrastructure and services include GitHub, Mailgun, Stripe, PayPal and OpenAI integrations.



What you need to bring



• Strong PHP experience

• Angular experience

• Solid JavaScript / TypeScript skills

• Good spoken and written English communication skills

• Willingness and ability to follow existing coding style

• Receptiveness to feedback and eagerness to learn

• Flexibility in adapting to our processes and structure

• Experience using GitHub for version control

• Long-term commitment (we hope our ideal applicant will work with us for several years)



Helpful additional experience



• Automated testing

• Laravel ecosystem tools

• Nginx environments

• CI/CD workflows

• Cloud infrastructure such as DigitalOcean

• Writing technical documentation



What the role involves



You’ll work on backend feature development, frontend implementation using Angular, code review, testing, bug fixing, improving existing functionality and documentation writing.



This role suits someone who wants a long-term role and enjoys hands-on development, being challenged to write clean, efficient and scalable code, continuing to learn and contributing at a higher technical level.



As you become familiar with the codebase and demonstrate your ability to follow the existing style and write clean, scalable code, you will move on to more complex tasks and increased responsibility.



The successful applicant will be self-motivated, have strong prioritisation skills and be open to feedback and ongoing learning.



Application process

	1.	Application review

	2.	Interview

	3.	Live coding trial (you will complete a short trial task while screensharing with our senior developer)



Important



Please do not use AI tools when writing your application. We want to see natural English, and small mistakes are completely fine.



Following instructions carefully is very important for this role.



When submitting your application:



Please use the following as the subject line of your application
:



Long-Term PHP + Angular Developer Application



At the start of your message, please write:



“I confirm that I read the instructions and that I did not use AI to write this application.”



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.



To apply



Please specify which of the technologies listed above you have working knowledge and experience in.



In addition to outlining your experience in relation to the skills mentioned above, please answer the following questions in your application.

	1.	How many years of experience do you have working with PHP?

	2.	How many years of experience do you have working with Angular?

	3.	What do you think is most important when working with an existing codebase written by another developer?

	4.	When you join a new project with an existing codebase, what do you typically do during your first week?

	5.	How would you describe the kind of code you aim to write? What principles guide your coding style?

	6.	When reviewing or modifying existing code, what do you usually look for first?

	7.	When you see code that works but is messy or difficult to understand, how do you d
 whether to leave it as it is or refactor it? Please explain your thinking.

	8.	Developers today often use AI tools when coding (for example ChatGPT, Copilot or similar tools). Please describe if and how you currently use AI when developing software, including when you find it helpful and when you prefer not to use it.

	9.	Provide a GitHub profile or code samples if available.

	10.	Confirm that you are able to work between 9am and 5pm Manila time.

	11.	How many hours per week are you currently available to work, and how many hours would you ideally prefer to work per week on an ongoing basis?

title: Automotive Operations & Dispatch Coordinator
description:
Key Responsibilities



1. Fleet & Retail Ops Coordination

*Own the estimate-to-approval-to-route workflow:

*Create and submit estimates

*Follow up for approval

*Ensure parts are sourced

*Finalize tech routes for the following day

*Track all jobs in progress and flag issues proactively

*Ensure routes are realistic and efficient; confirm techs are prepped and ready

*Communicate clearly with customers and fleet managers about job timing, status, and follow-ups

*Submit and close out fleet approvals in Element, LeasePlan, and AutoIntegrate

*Ensure invoices match approvals; follow up on outstanding invoices and missing payments



2. Scheduling & Productivity Tracking

*Maintain the daily technician schedule, making updates as needed

*Monitor tech productivity; flag idle time, reschedules, or delays

*Track time logs and coordinate with bookkeeper for payroll



3. Customer Communication

*Respond to incoming texts, WhatsApp messages, and system inquiries in a timely, professional manner

*Follow up with clients as needed to resolve open issues or gather job-related info

*Escalate concerns appropriately and promptly



4. Admin & Reporting

*Submit a detailed Daily Recap covering completed work, pending tasks, blockers, and KPIs

*Keep Trello and other tools up-to-date with accurate job and task statuses

*Track inventory needs and coordinate ordering of parts, oil, and supplies

*Monitor A/R and follow up on past-due fleet invoices



5. Marketing & Content Support

*Publish our monthly newsletter in MailChimp with content from marketing team

*Coordinate publishing of content to Instagram /
 / Google My Business

*Other light admin support as needed for the marketing team



What Success Looks Like

*All work orders, estimates, approvals, and tech routes are handled with zero dropped balls

*You identify blockers or problems before they become fires

*Communication is clear, timely, and professional — with both customers and internal team

*The business runs like a machine — nothing gets missed or delayed

*You take full ownership of your responsibilities and require very little follow-up



Who You Are

*A natural systems thinker who thrives on clarity, organization, and ownership

*Extremely proactive — you don’t wait to be told what to do

*Excellent at written and verbal English communication

*Comfortable in the automotive world and eager to learn the details

*Sharp and reliable — you retain info, spot gaps, and follow through



Experience & Skills Required

*3+ years of experience in a remote automotive: coordinator, dispatcher, operations role

*Demonstrated responsibility for keeping things moving without handholding

*Excellent written and spoken English

*Experience with dispatch, route planning, or technician scheduling

*Familiar with tools like Slack, Google Docs, Trello (or similar), CRM/invoicing platforms

*Experience working with U.S.-based field service, auto, or fleet companies

*Bonus: Familiarity with AutoIntegrate, Housecall Pro, Element, or LeasePlan platforms



Required Software Experience

*Slack

*WhatsApp

*Trello (or similar)

*Google Workspace

*CRM / invoicing tools (Housecall Pro, AutoIntegrate)

*
 (plus)



TECH REQUIREMENTS:

*Computer - at least i5 or equivalent with 8gb RAM

*Internet Speed - At least 50 MBPS Download speed

*Dual monitor setup is required to ensure efficiency in managing multiple applications simultaneously.

*Location: Remote (Philippines)

*Schedule: Part-time, US hours

Tuesday 1-5 pm

Wednesday 1-5 pm

Friday 8:30 - 7:30 pm

Saturday 10-4 pm

Sunday 2-5 pm



Pay: Php288.46 - Php432.69 hourly rate



Application Instructions:

Fill out this form: 
/appqNAvkEQ10YFlc3/pagrX4PvZe0xsKHWT/form

After submitting the form, click the apply button and send us a short message on why you would be a good fit for the role.

title: Lead Generator
description:
We are seeking a motivated and results-driven Lead Generator to join our team on a commission-based arrangement. As a Lead Generator, you will play a critical role in identifying and qualifying potential leads for our products/services. Your primary objective will be to generate high-quality leads that have the potential to convert into valuable clients. This role offers uncapped earning potential, where your success will be directly reflected in your commission earnings.



Our services start at $500-$25K and you will earn 10% off of every lead that is closed.



Prospecting and Outreach: Utilize various channels such as cold calling,
 campaigns, social media, and networking to identify and connect with potential leads.



Lead Qualification: Conduct thorough research on potential leads to ensure they meet the predefined criteria for your target audience. Qualify leads based on their needs, interests, and potential to convert.



Engagement: Initiate meaningful conversations with leads, showcasing a clear understanding of our products/services and how they can benefit the potential client. Maintain a professional and persuasive demeanor throughout interactions.



Effective Communication: Clearly and concisely convey the value proposition of our offerings, addressing any questions or objections the leads may have.



Pipeline Management: Maintain accurate records of interactions, lead status, and relevant details using CRM software. Keep track of follow-ups and nurture leads through the sales funnel.



Collaboration: Work closely with the sales team to ensure a smooth transition of qualified leads, providing them with the necessary information for successful conversions.



Performance Metrics: Meet or exceed monthly lead generation targets. Regularly analyze your performance, identify areas of improvement, and implement strategies to enhance your lead generation efforts.



Market Insights: Stay up-to-date with industry trends, competitive landscape, and customer needs to adjust your approach and messaging accordingly.



To apply please put: generate in the subject line

title: GHL CRM Setup - Fixed Price Gig
description:
This is not a standard GHL setup. We are a real Australian business with 3 state teams, 3 customer types, and 2 completely separate pipelines.

We run a single GHL account (not sub-accounts).

All 3 states operate within one account with smart list filtering, state-based routing, and full cross-state visibility.

The scope is detailed and specific. A full Build Brief is provided to shortlisted candidates.

The highest-priority build is a service pre-authorisation system — this is costing us money every week.

Google Reviews: all 3 Google Business profiles (VIC, QLD, NSW) are already connected to GHL and linked to the correct state entities. We have SMS and
 templates ready. We need triggers and workflows built only.



Do not apply if you cannot deliver the full scope. Do not apply if you need to learn GHL on the job.



About Xtracted Espresso Solutions

We are a family-owned, third-generation Australian coffee equipment business with 20+ years in the industry. We are the exclusive Australian importers of Synesso Coffee Machines, Bellwether Electric Roasters, Latte Art Factory, and Fiamma Coffee Machines. We run a commercial service division across Melbourne, Brisbane, and Sydney



Why We're Hiring

We have built a 20-year business on reputation and word of mouth. Our systems have not kept pace. Right now:

• Leads arrive and we quote once — no follow-up, no automation, no nurture

• Service division completes jobs without mandatory pre-authorisation — losing tens of thousands per year

• Three states operate with no consistent process — every customer gets a different experience

• Google Business profiles are connected in GHL and review templates are written — but no workflow or trigger exists to send them

• Website chatbot captures name and
 and stops — nothing fires after submission

• Existing customers receive zero re-engagement, no reminders, no check-ins — they go cold

• No visibility on pipeline value, lead source performance, or state-by-state reporting



Full Project Scope

Read every section carefully before applying.



1 — Account Foundation & Technical Setup

Account Structure — Already D
: Single Account

One GHL account for all 3 states. No sub-accounts. This decision is made.

All staff across VIC, QLD, and NSW operate in the same account with full cross-state visibility.

Leads are tagged by state and routed to the correct state team. Smart lists filter the daily work view per state.

Your job is to configure this correctly — not to recommend a different structure.



• Phone number provisioning — one dedicated number per state (VIC, QLD, NSW)

• A2P 10DLC SMS registration and compliance — must be completed before any SMS workflow goes live

•
 domain authentication — SPF, DKIM, DMARC configured and verified

• Account settings — timezone AEST, business branding, logo

• User profiles for all staff — role-based permissions, state assignment, full cross-state visibility

• Contact deduplication rules — merge logic for same contact arriving via multiple channels



2 — Two Separate Pipelines

Completely independent. A contact can exist in both but the journeys never overlap.



Pipeline 1 — Equipment Sales (11 stages) - this needs further thought

Covers machine sales, spare parts, Bellwether roasters, water filtration — all customer types. Opportunity value mandatory at Quote Sent for revenue forecasting.

STAGE PURPOSE

New Enquiry Lead arrived — not yet contacted

Contacted Initial outreach made

Discovery Call Booked Booking confirmed, calendar invite sent automatically

Quote Sent Proposal sent — 14-day follow-up begins, opportunity value recorded

Quote Follow-Up Automated sequence running

Verbal Confirmed Customer said yes — awaiting written confirmation

Invoice Sent Invoice raised and delivered

Payment Received Payment confirmed — installation triggered if required

Installation Booked Scheduled and confirmed to customer

Won & Complete Sale done — Google review request fires to correct state profile

Lost — Nurture Enters long-term re-engagement campaign



Pipeline 2 — Service Jobs (9 stages)

Covers all service bookings, repairs, maintenance, warranty. Pre-authorisation is mandatory — no exceptions.

STAGE PURPOSE

Service Request Received Form submitted — immediate acknowledgement fires

Approx Quote Sent Technician sends estimate + pre-auth link within 4 business hours

Pre-Auth Pending Awaiting customer to complete pre-authorisation

Pre-Auth Received Confirmed — job can now be scheduled

Job Scheduled Technician assigned, date and time confirmed to customer

On Site / In Progress Job underway

Job Complete — Invoice Sent Work done, invoice sent, payment taken from pre-auth

Paid & Closed Payment confirmed — Google review request fires to correct state profile

No Pre-Auth — Cancelled 72hrs no pre-auth — auto-cancelled, customer notified



?

3 — Custom Fields, Smart Lists & Opportunity Values

Custom Fields — All Contacts

FIELD OPTIONS / PURPOSE

Customer Type Cafe Owner / Restaurant / Coffee Roaster / Trade Technician / Other

State Victoria / Queensland / NSW / Other — primary routing field

Brand Interest Synesso / Bellwether / Fiamma / Latte Art Factory / Service / Spare Parts / Multiple

Budget Range Under $5k / $5k-$15k / $15k-$30k / $30k+ / Service Only

Business Name Trading name of their venue or business

Lead Source Instagram /
 
 / Chatbot / Website / Phone / Referral / Word of Mouth

Equipment Owned Current machines — critical for service history and upsell targeting

Last Contact Date Auto-updated on any interaction — drives 90-day re-engagement trigger

Job Type (Service) Repair / Maintenance / Installation / Warranty / Inspection

Pre-Auth Amount $1,000 / $2,000 / $3,000 / $5,000

Pre-Auth Status Pending / Received / Expired / Not Required



Smart Lists — Must Be Built and Saved

These are the daily working views for each state team. Not optional — they are what your staff open every morning instead of manually searching.

SMART LIST FILTER LOGIC

VIC — Open Sales Leads State = VIC + Sales Pipeline + Stage not Won or Lost

QLD — Open Sales Leads State = QLD + Sales Pipeline + Stage not Won or Lost

NSW — Open Sales Leads State = NSW + Sales Pipeline + Stage not Won or Lost

VIC — Active Service Jobs State = VIC + Service Pipeline + Stage not Paid or Cancelled

QLD — Active Service Jobs State = QLD + Service Pipeline + Stage not Paid or Cancelled

NSW — Active Service Jobs State = NSW + Service Pipeline + Stage not Paid or Cancelled

Pre-Auth Pending — All States Pre-Auth Status = Pending — owner-level view across all states

Quotes Sent — No Response 7+ Days Stage = Quote Sent + Last Contact Date older than 7 days

Cafe Owners by State Customer Type = Cafe Owner — filtered per state

Roasters — All States Customer Type = Coffee Roaster — combined view

No Contact in 90 Days Last Contact Date older than 90 days — existing customers only



Opportunity Value Tracking

• Opportunity value field mandatory when pipeline moves to Quote Sent

• Live pipeline value dashboard: total active quote value + breakdown by state and brand

• Won vs Lost value tracked month by month in reporting dashboard



4 — Nine Automation Workflows

Build and fully test in order. Each workflow confirmed end-to-end before moving to the next.



Workflow 1 — New Lead Instant Response

Trigger: any new lead via website form, chatbot, Instagram, or

• SMS sent within 2 minutes of submission

• Welcome
 sent simultaneously

• Contact created with all custom fields populated from form data

• Lead tagged by source and state — routed to correct state team automatically

• Staff notified: push notification + SMS +
 + internal task with 24hr deadline

• Lead enters Sales Pipeline at New Enquiry



Workflow 2 — Service Request Acknowledgement

Trigger: service booking form submitted

• Immediate auto-reply: 'We have your request — our team will be in touch within 4 business hours'

• Service job created in Service Pipeline at Service Request Received

• State technician notified via SMS +
 with full job details

• 4-hour internal task created — technician must send quote and pre-auth link within this window



Workflow 3 — Pre-Authorisation Enforcement

Highest Priority Build in the Entire Project

Our service division loses tens of thousands per year completing jobs without secured payment.

The rule: NO pre-authorisation = NO job booking. The system must enforce this — no manual override.

Pre-auth tiers: $1,000 / $2,000 / $3,000 / $5,000 — technician selects based on estimated scope.



• Pre-auth link sent to customer with explanation of why it is required

• 48 hours: if not completed — automated SMS +
 reminder fires

• 72 hours: if still not completed — automated cancellation sent to customer

• 72 hours: pipeline auto-moves to No Pre-Auth Cancelled, state manager notified

• On completion: instant confirmation to customer, pipeline moves to Pre-Auth Received, technician notified



Workflow 4 — 14-Day Sales Follow-Up Sequence

Trigger: pipeline moves to Quote Sent

• Day 1: SMS — short warm check-in

• Day 3:
 — value content relevant to their brand or equipment interest

• Day 7: SMS — brief follow-up

• Day 14:
 — final follow-up. No response = auto-move to Lost — Nurture



Workflow 5 — Long-Term Re-Engagement (Lost Leads)

Trigger: lead enters Lost — Nurture stage

• Monthly
 — industry content, product highlights, seasonal news

• Every 3 months — short personal SMS

• Stays in sequence indefinitely until re-engagement or unsubscribe



Workflow 6 — Google Review Automation (4 Triggers)

Google Business Profiles — Already Connected

All 3 Google Business profiles (VIC, QLD, NSW) are already linked to the correct state entities in GHL.

SMS and
 templates are already written by us — both supplied to the specialist on day one.

The specialist builds the triggers and workflows only. No profile setup or reconnection required.

The correct state profile must fire for each customer based on their State field — this must be tested explicitly.



Review Trigger A — Equipment Sale Won

• Trigger: Sales Pipeline moves to Won & Complete

• Day 0: SMS with Google review link — state profile matched automatically (VIC / QLD / NSW)

• Day 3: If no review — follow-up
 sent

• Day 7: If still no review — final SMS, sequence ends



Review Trigger B — Service Job Paid & Closed

• Trigger: Service Pipeline moves to Paid & Closed

• Same 3-step sequence — correct state profile fires automatically



Review Trigger C — Installation Completed

• Trigger: Sales Pipeline at Installation Booked + installation date has passed

• Same 3-step sequence — correct state profile fires automatically



Review Trigger D — Spare Parts Order Fulfilled

• Trigger: custom field Order Fulfilled marked on contact

• Same 3-step sequence — correct state profile fires automatically



Workflow 7 — Post-Sale & Post-Job Nurture

Separate from review requests — relationship-building touchpoints after the transaction closes.

• After equipment sale: Day 7 setup tips 
) / Day 30 check-in 
) / Day 90 first service reminder (SMS +
)

• After service job: Day 7 all working? (SMS) / Day 180 preventative maintenance reminder 
)



Workflow 8 — Service Job Reminders

Trigger: pipeline moves to Job Scheduled

• Immediate: booking confirmation SMS +

• 24 hours before: reminder SMS +

• 2 hours before: day-of reminder SMS



Workflow 9 — Missed Call Text-Back

Trigger: inbound call to any Xtracted number goes unanswered

• SMS fires within 60 seconds — war
ssage, offer to reply by text

• Internal task created — state staff to call back within 1 hour



Additional Automation — 90-Day Existing Customer Re-Engagement

This fires on existing customers who have gone cold — no pipeline movement and no contact in 90 days.

Trigger: Last Contact Date field exceeds 90 days with no activity on record.

Action: short personal SMS — Hi [name], it has been a while — just checking in on how the [equipment] is going.

Purpose: surface dormant customers and generate service bookings, upgrades, and parts orders on autopilot.

The No Contact in 90 Days smart list must be built alongside this workflow for manual review by the owner.



?

5 — Reputation Management

GHL's reputation tools configured so we never need to log into Google separately to monitor or respond to reviews.

• Reputation management tab configured — all 3 Google Business profiles visible in a single view

• New review notification —
 alert to state manager when a review is posted on their profile

• Review response — state manager can respond to Google reviews directly from within GHL



6 — Calendar & Booking System

• Connect Google Calendar for each staff member — 2-way sync

• Availability windows, buffer times, and booking limits set per person per state

• Online discovery call booking — customers book directly into correct state staff calendar

• Booking confirmation SMS +
 fires automatically on booking

• 24-hour and 1-hour reminders automated

• No-show follow-up triggered if call is not attended



7 — Social Media Connections & Chatbot Rebuild

Social Media

• Connect Instagram Business account — DMs visible in GHL conversations tab

• Connect
 Business page — Messenger DMs visible in GHL

• Auto-reply to all Instagram and
 
 — warm greeting, response time, booking link

• All social leads tagged by source and routed to correct state team



Website Chatbot Rebuild — 8-Step Qualifying Flow

Currently captures name and
 and stops. Must be rebuilt to qualify, tag, and route leads into GHL automatically on submission.

STEP ACTION

1 Greeting — equipment / service / spare parts?

2 Business name

3 Which state? — auto-assigns to correct state team

4 Brand or equipment interest — shows options

5 Rough budget range — shows options

6
 and mobile number

7 Book a discovery call now or prefer a callback?

8 Thank you — confirms team will be in touch within 1 business day



On submission: contact created in GHL with all fields populated, Workflow 1 fires automatically.



8 — Lead Source & Referral Tracking

• Lead Source custom field auto-populated from all entry points — chatbot, Instagram,
, website, phone, referral

• UTM parameter capture — any tracked or paid link populates the Lead Source field automatically

• Lead source report in dashboard — volume and conversion rate by source, updated in real time

• Month-on-month source comparison visible in reporting view



9 — Reporting Dashboard

Sales View

• Active leads by stage, by state, by brand, by customer type

• Total active pipeline value — live revenue forecast from opportunity values

• Conversion rates: New Enquiry to Quote Sent to Won

• Lost leads this month vs last month



Service View

• Service requests received vs jobs booked vs pre-auth conversion rate

• Jobs cancelled due to no pre-auth — this number must trend toward zero

• Completed jobs by state, average job value



Reviews View

• Google reviews generated this month — by trigger type and by state profile

• Review request SMS open rate and link click rate



Lead Source View

• Leads by source this month — volume and conversion

• Best performing source by state



Deliverables — Sign-Off Checklist

Final payment is not released until every item below is confirmed working end-to-end.



DELIVERABLE COMPLETE WHEN...

Technical foundation Phones live, A2P approved,
 authenticated, users and permissions set

Contact deduplication Merge logic tested — same contact via 2 channels = 1 record

Sales Pipeline — 11 stages All stages built, opportunity value mandatory at Quote Sent

Service Pipeline — 9 stages All stages built, pre-auth tiers configured

All custom fields All fields built and populating correctly from every entry point

Smart lists — 11 saved views All built, filtered correctly, visible to correct teams

Pre-auth workflow Full cycle tested — 72hr auto-cancel confirmed with dummy contact

Workflow 1 — New lead response Dummy lead receives SMS +
 within 2 minutes

Workflow 2 — Service acknowledgement Acknowledgement fires, technician notified, 4hr task created

Workflow 4 — Sales follow-up All 4 touchpoints confirmed in test contact timeline

Workflow 5 — Lost lead re-engagement Monthly
 + quarterly SMS confirmed in workflow

Workflow 6 — Reviews: Trigger A (Won) SMS Day 0 fires to correct state Google profile

Workflow 6 — Reviews: Trigger B (Service paid) SMS Day 0 fires to correct state Google profile

Workflow 6 — Reviews: Trigger C (Installation) SMS Day 0 fires after installation date passes

Workflow 6 — Reviews: Trigger D (Parts fulfilled) SMS Day 0 fires on Order Fulfilled field update

Review Day 3 + Day 7 follow-ups
 Day 3 and SMS Day 7 confirmed for all 4 triggers

Workflow 7 — Post-sale nurture Day 7 / 30 / 90 and Day 7 / 180 sequences confirmed

Workflow 8 — Job reminders Confirmation, 24hr, 2hr reminders tested end-to-end

Workflow 9 — Missed call text-back SMS fires within 60 seconds of missed call

90-day re-engagement trigger Tested on dummy contact with backdated Last Contact Date

Reputation management All 3 Google profiles visible in GHL, notifications on, response working

Calendar & booking All staff calendars live, booking page tested, reminders firing

Instagram +
 connected 
 visible in 
, auto-reply tested

Chatbot rebuilt and live 8-step flow live on website, contact created in GHL on submit, Workflow 1 fires

Lead source tracking UTM capture working, source field populating from all channels

Reporting dashboard — 4 views Sales, service, reviews, and lead source all live with accurate data

Handover document Plain English — what was built, how to maintain and update it

Loom walkthrough video Full account walkthrough for staff training — minimum 20 minutes



Payment & Timeline

PAYMENT 

Total fixed price AUD $1,600

Deposit AUD $500 via Wise — paid before work begins

Final payment AUD $1100 on completion — released when full sign-off checklist is confirmed

Method Wise (preferred) or direct bank transfer



WEEK FOCUS

Week 1 Foundation + both pipelines + custom fields + 11 smart lists + user setup + pre-auth workflow

Week 2 All 9 workflows + Google review automation (4 triggers, 3 state profiles) + 90-day re-engagement + post-sale nurture

Week 3 Calendar setup + social connections + chatbot rebuild + reputation management + lead source tracking

Week 4 End-to-end testing of every deliverable + reporting dashboard + handover document + Loom walkthrough



Skills Required

Non-Negotiable — Must Have All of These



GoHighLevel: minimum 2 years hands-on experience building complex multi-step workflow automations.

A2P 10DLC SMS registration: must have completed this process before — no exceptions.

 domain authentication: SPF, DKIM, DMARC. Must have configured this before.

Smart lists and custom fields: able to build complex filtered views and conditional field logic.

GHL chatbot: multi-step qualifying conversation design and trigger setup.

Payment or pre-auth integration in GHL: Stripe, GHL Payments, or equivalent — must have built this before.

Strong written English and clear daily communication with business owner.

Able to work independently from a detailed brief with minimal supervision.



Valued — Strong Advantage

•	Experience with service-based or trade businesses — field service, equipment, repairs

•	GHL Certified Admin credentials

•	Google Business Profile and reputation management experience inside GHL

•	Zapier or Make for integrations where native connections fall short

•	Meta Ads Manager experience — being added to ongoing retainer scope

•	Experience working with Australian businesses



How to Apply

Your application must include all five items below. Missing any single one means we will not review it.



Application Checklist — All 5 Required

1.  SCREEN RECORDING (2-3 min): Walk through a complex GHL workflow you have built. Show the trigger, any conditions or branches, and what fires at the end. Loom link preferred.

2.  PRE-AUTH OR PAYMENT EXPERIENCE: Describe specifically how you have handled a payment, pre-authorisation, or deposit workflow inside GHL. What tool did you use and how did the pipeline stage change work?

3.  WRITTEN ANSWER: 'A cafe owner submits our website chatbot at 10pm on a Friday. Walk me through exactly what happens in GHL over the next 14 days — every touchpoint, every automation, every pipeline movement.'

4.  YOUR PROPOSAL: A week-by-week build plan and confirmation that AUD $1,600 fixed price works for you.

5.  AVAILABILITY: Confirm you can start within 7 days and have at least 3 hours of daily overlap with Australian Eastern Time.

title: AU Tax Accountant
description:
We believe our people are the foundation of our success. Our culture is built on respect, collaboration, and supporting one another.



We strive to create a workplace where employees feel valued, heard, and empowered to grow both personally and professionally. Our leadership is committed to fostering open communication, professional development, and a positive environment where everyone can perform at their best.



If you're looking for a company that values your contribution and supports your growth, we’d love to have you on our team.



Why Join Bravo?

We believe great people deserve great support. Here’s what you can expect when you join us:

-Competitive and stable full-time work from home opportunity

-HMO and Life Insurance coverage

-13th Month Pay

-Government Contributions (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG)

-Sick Leave and Vacation Leave benefits

-Free Training and Professional Development opportunities

-Monthly Virtual Employee Engagement activities (with prizes!)

-Fun Company Get-Togethers

-An Annual Christmas Party that our team truly looks forward to

-Year-End Package



We make sure our team not only works hard, but also feels appreciated and celebrated.



About the Role

We are currently looking for four (4) experienced AU Tax Accountants to join our growing team.

In this role, you will work closely with an Australian firm handling tax compliance and accounting services for a diverse client base. This position offers the opportunity to further develop your expertise in Australian taxation while working in a supportive and collaborative environment.



Key Responsibilities

-Prepare and review Australian Tax Returns, BAS, and Financial Statements

-Ensure compliance with Australian tax regulations and legislation

-Provide tax planning support or advice to clients or internal stakeholders

-Analyze financial statements and provide insights where needed

-Maintain accurate financial records and documentation



What We’re Looking For

-2–5+ years of Australian Tax and Business Services experience

-Ability to prepare tax returns for Individuals, Partnerships, Companies (including Group Companies), and Trusts

-Experience in preparing and reviewing Financial Statements

-Solid understanding of Australian tax legislation, including:

Capital Gains Tax (CGT)

Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT)

Division 7A compliance and application

-Experience in preparing IAS and BAS

-Experience in tax planning is an advantage

-Strong analytical and communication skills

-Proficient in using Xero, XPM, and other accounting software

-Positive and proactive attitude with a fun team spirit

-Bookkeeping experience is a plus



If you're interested in this opportunity, feel free to apply through this job post or send your resume directly to 
 look forward to hearing from you!

title: Sales Operations Executive
description:
Base 60k php/month with performance bonus + wellness day off after probation



We’re looking for a proactive and highly communicative Sales Operations Executive to support our Growth Ops team and ensure smooth operations across our done-for-you (DFY) sales and marketing clients.



This role is not just about admin tasks—it’s about being the glue that keeps sales pods running, supporting closers and setters, and making sure nothing slips through the cracks. If you thrive in a fast-paced, people-focused environment and love keeping teams organized and accountable, this role is for you!



This is a full-time position and requires working 8am - 4pm Pacific Time Zone with solid wi-fi, power and backup.

You must complete DISC and IQ assessment and add that to your profile before applying.



________



Detailed Job Description Breakdown



Sales Pod Support & Admin Operations

- Assist Closer and Setters Manager in day-to-day admin needs to keep the sales pod running smoothly.

- Attend tea
etings and huddles, track action items, and ensure follow-through.

- Onboard and offboard new closers and setters with proper systems, documentation, and SOP access.

- Maintain sales documentation and assets within client accounts for easy retrieval.



Accountability & Compliance

- Audit closer calendars for availability and ensure SOPs are being followed.

- Hold closers and setters accountable to submit end-of-day reports and complete post-call dispositions.

- Conduct call audits and sales data audits to maintain accuracy and alignment.



Cross-Team Collaboration

- Work closely with Front-End and Back-End Tech Ops Specialists to resolve discrepancies in sales data or workflow.

- Communicate updates clearly to stakeholders and ensure expectations are aligned.

- Support closers and setters (who may be tech-challenged or impatient) with patience, persistence, and strong communication.



________



Skills & Qualifications

- 1+ year experience in sales admin, executive assistant, or similar role.

- Must be excellent in verbal and written communication (comfortable jumping on calls and video huddles).

- Strong interpersonal skills: you know how to chase for updates and hold people accountable while remaining empathetic, professional, and respectful.

- Proactive and solutions-driven—you don’t just flag problems, you bring answers.

- Organized and detail-oriented—able to keep track of multiple requests without dropping the ball.

- Experience working with salespeople/closers is highly preferred.

- Thrives in fast-paced, people-driven environments.



If you’re looking for a role where you can combine organizational excellence, proactive communication, and accountability in a high-impact sales environment, we’d love to hear from you!



________



About Sales Possible



At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching businesses to scale their impact through world-class marketing, sales systems, and team training.



We don’t just teach sales, we build full-funnel, sustainable systems that empower both businesses and the people behind them.



Our clients range from expert coaches to fast-growing education brands, and we take pride in helping them turn vision into real results.



We’re a tight-knit, fast-moving team that values clear communication, ownership, and continuous growth. If you’re someone who thrives in a proactive, performance-driven environment and wants to build cutting-edge AI & automation systems, you’ll feel right at home here.

title: Lead Conversion Specialist / Appointment Setter – Marketing Agency Clients
description:
About Us

GROM Digital is a marketing agency specialising in aesthetic clinics and medical spas.

We generate consultation leads for clinics through digital marketing, and we are looking for a Lead Conversion Specialist to help turn those enquiries into booked appointments.



Role Overview

You will communicate with leads who have expressed interest in treatments such as Botox, fillers, skin treatments, and body procedures.



Your job is to respond quickly, answer basic questions, and book them directly into the clinic’s calendar.



Responsibilities

• Respond to incoming leads quickly (within 5–10 minutes)

• Communicate with leads via SMS, WhatsApp,
, and Instagram

• Answer common questions about treatments

• Qualify potential patients

• Book consultations into clinic calendars

• Collect deposits where required

• Follow up with leads who have not booked

• Update CRM notes and lead status

• Maintain friendly, professional communication



Requirements

• Excellent written English

• Experience in customer service or appointment setting

• Strong communication skills

• Ability to respond quickly and professionally

• Comfortable using CRM systems and messaging platforms

• Highly organised and reliable



Experience in medical, aesthetics, or sales is a bonus but not required.



Working Hours

40 hours per week.

Must be available to respond to leads quickly during assigned working hours.



Compensation

$700 – $900 per month depending on experience.



Performance bonuses may be available based on booking performance.



How to Apply

Please include:

Your previous customer service or appointment setting experience

A short voice recording introducing yourself

Your internet speed

The word “CONVERSION” at the top of your application

 As the Customer Service Coordinator for the Commercial Department, you’ll serve as the go-to person for all customer interactions and internal coordination related to our commercial operations.  to stay organized and connected.

Experience

Skills

title: Real Estate Cold Caller
description:
About Our Company



—Premier Path Property Group is a U.S.-based real estate investment company that specializes in buying off-market properties directly from homeowners. Our team focuses on finding motivated sellers and helping them sell their properties quickly for cash.



We are looking for a motivated and reliable Cold Caller to reach out to homeowners, start conversations, and identify people interested in selling their property.



Job Responsibilities

—Your primary responsibility will be calling property owners from a provided list and identifying motivated sellers.



Daily tasks include:

 • Making outbound cold calls to homeowners using our dialer

 • Following a simple script to start conversations

 • Asking qualifying questions about the property and the seller’s situation

 • Identifying motivated sellers interested in selling

 • Setting appointments or passing qualified leads to our acquisitions team

 • Updating call results and notes in our CRM

 • Meeting daily and weekly call targets



Requirements

—We are looking for someone who is:

 • Fluent in English with a neutral accent

 • Comfortable making 100–300+ calls per day

 • Experienced in cold calling or telemarketing

 • Reliable with a strong internet connection

 • Familiar with real estate or wholesaling (preferred but not required)

 • Confident talking to homeowners and handling objections



Experience with these tools is a plus:

 • CRM systems

 • Auto dialers

 • Real estate lead generation



Compensation

—We offer competitive pay and incentives.



Base Pay: Hourly

Bonuses: Paid for qualified leads and/or closed deals.



Top performers can earn significant bonuses each month.





What Success Looks Like



A successful cold caller will:

 • Generate consistent motivated seller leads

 • Maintain a positive attitude on calls

 • Be reliable and consistent with call volume

 • Communicate clearly with our acquisitions team



How to Apply

—Please include the following in your application:

 1. A short voice recording introducing yourself

 2. Your previous cold calling experience

 3. Your internet speed test

 4. Your availability in EST



We look forward to working with you!

title: Part-Time Real Estate Cold Caller (25 Hours per Week)
description:
Kainos Ventures LLC – Real Estate Investment & Acquisitions Company



About Kainos Ventures LLC

Kainos Ventures LLC is a U.S.-based real estate investment company specializing in wholesaling, fix-and-flip projects, and creative financing strategies such as subject-to and seller financing deals. We focus on helping homeowners across the U.S. find fast and fair solutions while building a portfolio of investment properties. Our mission is to create win-win opportunities and empower our team members to grow professionally in the real estate industry.



Job Title:

Part-Time Real Estate Cold Caller (25 Hours per Week)



Company Overview:

Kainos Ventures LLC is expanding its acquisitions team and looking for a motivated, reliable, and professional Cold Caller to generate qualified seller leads through outbound calls. The ideal candidate will have excellent English communication skills, a strong work ethic, and a passion for real estate.



Position Type:

Part-Time (25 hours per week)

Remote / Work-from-Home

Flexible Schedule: Choose from one of two shifts:

10:00 AM – 3:00 PM EST (11:00 PM – 4:00 AM PH Time)

4:00 PM – 8:00 PM EST (5:00 AM – 9:00 AM PH Time)



Job Summary:

The Cold Caller will contact property owners across the U.S., engage in meaningful conversations, collect essential property information, and schedule follow-up appointments for our acquisitions team. This position requires persistence, excellent communication skills, and the ability to handle rejection professionally.



Key Responsibilities:

- Make 300+ outbound calls per day using the company’s dialing system.



- Follow provided scripts and objection-handling guides to qualify leads.



- Gather property details such as motivation, condition, timeline, and asking price.



- Enter accurate notes and lead data into the CRM (REISimpli or similar).



- Schedule appointments for acquisitions managers.



- Track and report daily/weekly progress including leads generated.



- Participate in team meetings and training sessions for performance growth.





Required Qualifications:

- At least 1 year of cold calling, telemarketing, or inside sales experience (real estate preferred).



- Excellent spoken English with a clear or neutral accent.



- Comfortable using scripts, CRMs, and dialer software.



- Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace with headset.



- Self-motivated, goal-driven, and able to work independently.



- High school diploma or equivalent required (some college preferred).





Preferred Skills:

- Experience in real estate acquisitions or wholesaling.



- Familiarity with tools such as ReadyMode, REISimpli, Google Sheets, and Slack.



- Understanding of real estate terms (ARV, equity, repairs, motivated seller leads, etc.).



- Excellent rapport-building and follow-up skills.



- Positive attitude and resilience under rejection.





Compensation:

- Hourly Rate: $4.50 – $6.00 USD/hour (?265 – ?355/hour)



- Performance Bonuses: Based on qualified leads and closed contracts.



- Training Provided with opportunities for advancement.





Performance Metrics:

- Total calls made per shift



- Talk time and connection rate



- Qualified leads generated



- Appointment setting rate



- CRM accuracy and completeness





???? What You’ll Gain:

- Comprehensive training in U.S. real estate cold calling and acquisitions.



- Performance-based bonuses and growth potential into lead manager or acquisitions roles.



- Work-from-home flexibility with a professional and supportive team.



- A chance to build long-term skills in communication, negotiation, and real estate investing.

title: Lead Generation Team Leader – Build & Run Lead Gen Operation - COMMISSION BASED
description:
I am building a lead generation business and I am looking for an experienced Lead Generation Team Leader who wants to help build and run the operational side of the business.



I want to be transparent: I am the business owner, but I am not a lead generation expert. My focus will be on finding and closing clients.



The person in this role will help build and manage the lead generation operation.



This includes building the workflow, organizing systems, managing workers, maintaining lead quality, and ensuring that leads meet client requirements.



This is not a simple VA task role. I am looking for someone who can take ownership of the operational side of the business and treat it like something we are building together.



Responsibilities



• Organize and build the lead generation workflow

• Research leads across different industries

• Manage leads inside CRM systems

• Maintain lead quality and verification

• Train and guide lead generation workers

• Track and organize leads before they are delivered to clients

• Improve systems and processes as the business grows



The initial focus will be
-based lead generation, with the potential to expand into cold calling and other outreach methods later.



Compensation Structure



This role is commission-based.



You will receive 10% of the profit generated by the lead generation operation.



The role will start part-time while the system and clients are being built, with the potential to grow into full-time as the business expands.



This structure is designed for someone who is confident in their ability and wants to help build something long-term, rather than someone looking only for a fixed hourly job.



The Type of Person I Am Looking For



Someone who:



• Has real experience with lead generation

• Can build systems and organize processes

• Is comfortable managing and training workers

• Can adapt strategies for different industries

• Takes initiative and solves problems independently

• Has a leadership mindset and ownership mentality



How To Apply (Important)



Please read carefully.



Due to the number of applications, I may not respond to messages in the OnlineJobs inbox.



If you are interested, please follow these steps:



Step 1

Listen to the full explanation of this opportunity here:





Step 2

Send a WhatsApp message to: 
Your message must include:



• The words “I listened to the recording.”

• A short video introducing yourself and explaining why you believe you are a good fit for this role



Step 3



Request to join the interview update group here:





If your message and video stand out, you will be approved into the group, where updates about the interview and next steps will be posted.



This is an opportunity for someone who wants responsibility, ownership, and the chance to help build a growing business. Looking forward to hearing from you !
